audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Dinah, Shechem/Hamor negotiation, brothers' deceit, circumcision demand, Simeon/Levi retaliation, plunder, and Jacob's fear. Sensitive wording is restrained while preserving the source's moral stakes.

verses.restored: Dinah, daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Jacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.

familiar.text: And Dinah the daughter of Leah... went out to see the daughters of the land.

verses.restored: Shechem son of Hamor the Hivite, prince of the land, saw her, took her, lay with her, and humbled her.

familiar.text: And when Shechem the son of Hamor... saw her, he took her...

notes.title: Humbled her
notes.body: Anah is severe in this context and marks harm against Dinah. The sequence saw, took, lay, humbled is not framed as mutual courtship.

verses.restored: His soul clung to Dinah daughter of Jacob. He loved the young woman and spoke to the heart of the young woman.

familiar.text: And his soul clave unto Dinah...

notes.title: Love after harm
notes.body: The text uses love language after the harm already named; it does not undo the first act.

verses.restored: Shechem said to Hamor his father, Take this girl for me as a woman.

familiar.text: And Shechem spake unto his father Hamor...

verses.restored: Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter, but his sons were with his livestock in the field, so Jacob kept silent until they came.

familiar.text: And Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah his daughter...

notes.title: Defiled
notes.body: Tame names dishonor within the social world of the text; it must not be read as blame on Dinah.

verses.restored: Hamor father of Shechem went out to Jacob to speak with him.

familiar.text: And Hamor the father of Shechem went out unto Jacob...

verses.restored: The sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard. The men were pained and very angry, because he had done disgrace in Israel by lying with Jacob’s daughter, for such a thing should not be done.

familiar.text: And the sons of Jacob came out of the field when they heard it...

notes.title: Disgrace in Israel
notes.body: Nebalah means a shameful outrage. Israel here is family identity before later national identity.

verses.restored: Hamor spoke with them, saying, The soul of my son Shechem longs for your daughter. Please give her to him as a woman.

familiar.text: And Hamor communed with them, saying...

verses.restored: Intermarry with us. Give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.

familiar.text: And make ye marriages with us...

verses.restored: Dwell with us, and the land shall be before you. Dwell and trade in it, and acquire holdings in it.

familiar.text: And ye shall dwell with us: and the land shall be before you...

verses.restored: Shechem said to her father and to her brothers, Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you say to me I will give.

familiar.text: And Shechem said unto her father and unto her brethren...

verses.restored: Set bride-price and gift upon me very heavily, and I will give whatever you say to me; only give me the young woman as a woman.

familiar.text: Ask me never so much dowry and gift...

verses.restored: The sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father with deceit, and spoke because he had defiled Dinah their sister.

familiar.text: And the sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father deceitfully...

verses.restored: They said to them, We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to a man who has foreskin, for that would be a disgrace to us.

familiar.text: And they said unto them, We cannot do this thing...

verses.restored: Only on this condition will we consent to you: if you become like us, every male among you being circumcised.

familiar.text: But in this will we consent unto you...

verses.restored: Then we will give our daughters to you, and your daughters we will take for ourselves, and we will dwell with you and become one people.

familiar.text: Then will we give our daughters unto you...

verses.restored: But if you will not listen to us to be circumcised, then we will take our daughter and go.

familiar.text: But if ye will not hearken unto us...

verses.restored: Their words were good in the eyes of Hamor and in the eyes of Shechem, Hamor’s son.

familiar.text: And their words pleased Hamor, and Shechem...

verses.restored: The young man did not delay to do the thing, because he delighted in Jacob’s daughter. He was honored above all his father’s house.

familiar.text: And the young man deferred not to do the thing...

verses.restored: Hamor and Shechem his son came to the gate of their city and spoke to the men of their city.

familiar.text: And Hamor and Shechem his son came unto the gate...

verses.restored: These men are at peace with us. Let them dwell in the land and trade in it, for the land is broad enough before them. We will take their daughters and give our daughters to them.

familiar.text: These men are peaceable with us...

verses.restored: Only on this condition will the men consent to us to dwell with us, to become one people: that every male among us be circumcised as they are circumcised.

familiar.text: Only herein will the men consent unto us...

verses.restored: Will not their livestock, their possessions, and all their animals be ours? Only let us consent to them, and they will dwell with us.

familiar.text: Shall not their cattle and their substance and every beast...

notes.title: Economic motive
notes.body: The proposal is sold to the city through access to Jacob’s wealth.

verses.restored: All who went out of the gate of his city listened to Hamor and Shechem his son, and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.

familiar.text: And unto Hamor and unto Shechem his son hearkened all...

verses.restored: On the third day, when they were in pain, two sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, each took his sword, came upon the city in confidence, and killed every male.

familiar.text: And it came to pass on the third day, when they were sore...

notes.title: Dinah’s brothers
notes.body: The narrative identifies Simeon and Levi by their relationship to Dinah, linking retaliation to family honor and outrage.

verses.restored: They killed Hamor and Shechem his son with the edge of the sword, took Dinah from Shechem’s house, and went out.

familiar.text: And they slew Hamor and Shechem his son...

verses.restored: The sons of Jacob came upon the slain and plundered the city, because they had defiled their sister.

familiar.text: The sons of Jacob came upon the slain, and spoiled the city...

verses.restored: They took their flocks, their herds, their donkeys, what was in the city and what was in the field.

familiar.text: They took their sheep, and their oxen, and their asses...

verses.restored: All their wealth, all their little ones, and their women they took captive and plundered, and all that was in the house.

familiar.text: And all their wealth, and all their little ones...

notes.title: Escalated collective violence
notes.body: The retaliation expands beyond Shechem and Hamor to the whole city and its households.

verses.restored: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, You have troubled me, making me stink among the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanite and the Perizzite. I am few in number, and if they gather against me and strike me, I and my house will be destroyed.

familiar.text: And Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Ye have troubled me...

verses.restored: They said, Should he treat our sister like a prostitute?

familiar.text: And they said, Should he deal with our sister as with an harlot?

notes.title: Final protest
notes.body: Simeon and Levi end by naming Dinah’s dishonor; the chapter leaves their response morally unresolved.
